segments.text: Then Eliphaz the Temanite answered and said:
If one attempts a word with you, will you become weary? But who can withhold himself from speaking?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Surely you have instructed many, And you have strengthened weak hands.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Your words have upheld him who was stumbling, And you have strengthened the feeble knees;
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: But now it comes upon you, and you are weary; It touches you, and you are troubled.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Is not your reverence your confidence? And the integrity of your ways your hope?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Remember now, who ever perished being innocent? Or where were the upright ever cut off?
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Even as I have seen, Those who plow iniquity And sow trouble reap the same.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: By the blast of God they perish, And by the breath of His anger they are consumed.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The roaring of the lion, The voice of the fierce lion, And the teeth of the young lions are broken.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: The old lion perishes for lack of prey, And the cubs of the lioness are scattered.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Now a word was secretly brought to me, And my ear received a whisper of it.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: In disquieting thoughts from the visions of the night, When deep sleep falls on men,
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Fear came upon me, and trembling, Which made all my bones shake.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Then a spirit passed before my face; The hair on my body stood up.
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: It stood still, But I could not discern its appearance. A form was before my eyes; There was silence; Then I heard a voice saying:
segments.speaker: Eliphaz

segments.text: Can a mortal be more righteous than God? Can a man be more pure than his Maker?
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: If He puts no trust in His servants, If He charges His angels with error,
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: How much more those who dwell in houses of clay, Whose foundation is in the dust, Who are crushed before a moth?
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: They are broken in pieces from morning till evening; They perish forever, with no one regarding.
segments.speaker: Spirit

segments.text: Does not their own excellence go away? They die, even without wisdom.
segments.speaker: Spirit
